engineGuide.h2: Toyota Venza Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Toyota Venza across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.7L Petrol
entries.history: The 1AR-FE brought efficient four-cylinder power to the Venza, sharing architecture with the Camry but tuned for crossover duty; reliable when maintained but prone to oil consumption.
compatibleVariants: 2.7L FWD Base, 2.7L FWD LE, 2.7L FWD XLE, 2.7L FWD Limited, 2.7L AWD LE, 2.7L AWD XLE, 2.7L AWD Limited
commonFailures: Piston ring wear causing excessive oil consumption (70k-120k miles)
Throttle body failure triggering P2109 code (facelift models)
MAF sensor contamination causing lean codes and poor idle
entries.cta: Get quotes for 1AR-FE Toyota Venza engine replacement

entries.title: 3.5L Petrol
entries.history: Toyota's acclaimed 2GR-FE V6 delivered smooth, powerful performance to the Venza; widely regarded as one of Toyota's most refined engines, though affected by a specific VVT-i issue.
compatibleVariants: 3.5L V6 FWD Base, 3.5L V6 FWD LE, 3.5L V6 FWD XLE, 3.5L V6 FWD Limited, 3.5L V6 FWD Sport, 3.5L V6 AWD LE, 3.5L V6 AWD XLE, 3.5L V6 AWD Limited
commonFailures: Exhaust VVT-i actuator bolt failure causing ticking noise (TSB T-SB-0094-09, 60k-100k miles)
Timing chain tensioner wear leading to rattle on cold start
Oil filter housing gasket leaks causing external oil seepage
entries.cta: Get quotes for 2GR-FE Toyota Venza engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5L Hybrid
entries.history: The A25A-FXS represents Toyota's latest hybrid architecture, combining a high-efficiency Atkinson-cycle petrol engine with dual electric motors for the second-gen Venza's AWD-e system.

entries.title: Hybrid LE AWD, 2.5L Hybrid XLE AWD, 2.5L Hybrid Limited AWD
commonFailures: EGR cooler internal seal degradation causing coolant loss (early production)
Electrical bypass valve faults affecting hybrid system operation
Hybrid battery cooling fan failure triggering system warnings
entries.cta: Get quotes for A25A-FXS Toyota Venza engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Toyota Venza Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.h4: Toyota Venza 2GR-FE VVT-i Ticking Noise - Why V6 Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.rootCause: Exhaust VVT-i gear actuator bolts loosen or shear, causing cam timing drift, metallic ticking from valve cover area, and DTCs P0014/P0024/P0017.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty exhaust VVT-i actuator and bolts per TSB T-SB-0094-09
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; intake actuator may fail next; 20-40k mile extension

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace both intake/exhaust actuators, timing chain, tensioners, guides
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses root cause; suitable if block/head are sound; 60-80k mile expectation

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt long block with upgraded VVT-i components, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most cost-effective for high-mileage Venza; eliminates all internal wear concerns

problems.recommendation: If your Venza has under 80k miles and the ticking is early-stage, the TSB actuator repair may suffice. However, once DTCs appear or noise is pronounced, replacement with a rebuilt 2GR-FE featuring upgraded VVT-i components typically offers better long-term value than repeated repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 2GR-FE Toyota Venza engine prices

problems.h4: Toyota Venza 1AR-FE Oil Consumption - Why 2.7L Engines Burn Oil
problems.rootCause: Piston ring design allows oil to pass into combustion chamber; exacerbated by short-trip driving and infrequent oil changes, leading to blue smoke and low oil warnings.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace PCV valve, valve cover gasket, oil seals
repairOptions.longevity: May reduce consumption if rings are not severely worn; temporary measure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine removal, head-off, ring replacement with revised design
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses root cause but labour-intensive; suitable if block is otherwise sound

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded piston rings, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable solution for high-mileage Venza; eliminates consumption and related damage

problems.recommendation: For Venza owners noticing oil consumption above 1L per 1,000 miles, replacement with a rebuilt 1AR-FE featuring revised piston rings is typically more economical than an in-situ ring job, especially when factoring in labour and potential collateral damage from oil starvation.
problems.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt 1AR-FE Toyota Venza engines

problems.group: Toyota Venza P2109 Throttle Actuator Code
problems.h4: Toyota Venza P2109 Throttle Actuator Code - 1AR-FE Electronic Failure
problems.rootCause: Throttle body motor or position sensor fails internally, triggering P2109 (throttle actuator control system - minimum stop position performance), causing limp mode, poor idle, or no-start.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Clean throttle plate, perform electronic relearn procedure
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if failure is carbon-related; ineffective for internal motor failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace throttle body assembly, perform adaptation
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if failure is isolated to throttle body; does not address other engine wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with new throttle body, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value when Venza has high mileage or multiple concurrent issues

problems.recommendation: If P2109 appears on a low-mileage Venza with otherwise good service history, throttle body replacement is cost-effective. However, on higher-mileage examples, investing in a rebuilt engine prevents future failures and provides warranty protection.
problems.cta: Find Toyota Venza 1AR-FE engine replacement deals

problems.group: Toyota Venza Hybrid A25A-FXS EGR Cooler Issues
problems.h4: Toyota Venza Hybrid A25A-FXS EGR Cooler Issues - Coolant Leak Risk
problems.rootCause: EGR cooler internal seals degrade, allowing coolant to enter exhaust or combustion chamber; may trigger coolant loss warnings, white smoke, or hybrid system faults.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove EGR cooler, replace seals/gaskets, pressure test
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if caught early; requires hybrid system expertise

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt A25A-FXS Hybrid Unit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt hybrid powertrain with updated EGR components, system calibration, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for confirmed cooler failure; ensures hybrid system integrity

problems.recommendation: Given the complexity and cost of hybrid system repairs, early diagnosis is critical. If coolant loss is confirmed and linked to the EGR cooler, replacement with a rebuilt A25A-FXS unit featuring updated components offers the most reliable long-term solution for Venza hybrid owners.
problems.cta: Enquire about Toyota Venza hybrid engine replacement

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Toyota Venza
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're facing VVT-i rattle on the 2GR-FE V6, oil consumption on the 1AR-FE 2.7L, or hybrid system concerns on the A25A-FXS, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Toyota Venza specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing known failure points, bench testing, and a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. With nationwide delivery and supply & fit options, get your crossover back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Toyota Venza Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Toyota Venza and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineYears.h2: Toyota Venza Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Toyota Venza's production history, organised so UK grey import owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from specialists.

years.description: The Venza launched as a Camry-based crossover with two petrol engines, quickly gaining popularity among UK grey import buyers seeking refined family transport.
keyChanges: Toyota Venza introduced to North American/Japanese markets; UK availability via grey import
Two engine options: 1AR-FE 2.7L four-cylinder and 2GR-FE 3.5L V6
FWD and AWD configurations available for both engines
AGV10 chassis code for 2.7L; GGV10 for 3.5L V6
mainEngines: 1AR-FE - 2.7L petrol (2.7L FWD/AWD Base, LE, XLE, Limited)
2GR-FE - 3.5L petrol (3.5L V6 FWD/AWD Base, LE, XLE, Limited, Sport)
knownFor: Smooth, refined driving experience typical of Toyota crossovers
Early 2GR-FE units (2009) affected by VVT-i actuator TSB T-SB-0094-09
1AR-FE oil consumption emerging on higher-mileage examples
Strong parts compatibility with Camry/Highlander aiding repairs
years.cta: Get quotes for 2008-2012 Toyota Venza replacement engines
years.ticker: Toyota Venza 2GR-FE ticking noise fix • Toyota Venza 1AR-FE oil consumption repair • Toyota Venza 2009 V6 engine replacement

years.description: The facelifted Venza received updated styling and minor engine revisions, though the core 1AR-FE and 2GR-FE powertrains continued with known failure patterns persisting.
keyChanges: Exterior refresh with new front/rear styling and LED accents
Chassis codes updated: AGV15 for 2.7L, GGV15 for 3.5L V6
Minor ECU revisions to address P2109 throttle body code on 1AR-FE
Final production year 2017 before model hiatus
mainEngines: 2GR-FE - 3.5L petrol (3.5L V6 FWD/AWD LE, XLE, Limited, Sport)
knownFor: Facelift styling more appealing to UK grey import buyers
2GR-FE VVT-i rattle still prevalent; upgraded actuators recommended on replacement
1AR-FE P2109 throttle body code more common on facelift models
Strong residual values supporting replacement investment decisions
years.cta: Compare Toyota Venza 2013-2017 engine replacement prices
years.ticker: Toyota Venza 2013 P2109 fix • Toyota Venza facelift V6 engine cost • Toyota Venza 2015 2.7L oil consumption
enquiries: Toyota Venza facelift V6 engine cost

years.description: The revived Venza returned exclusively as a hybrid, leveraging Toyota's latest A25A-FXS powertrain for efficiency-focused UK grey import buyers.
keyChanges: Venza nameplate revived on TNGA-K platform; hybrid-only powertrain
A25A-FXS 2.5L Dynamic Force hybrid with AWD-e electric rear motor
No petrol-only or diesel variant offered in any market
Premium positioning with standard AWD and advanced safety tech
knownFor: Excellent fuel economy for a midsize crossover (40+ mpg combined)
Early reports of EGR cooler seal issues on pre-2023 production
Hybrid system complexity requiring specialist diagnostic equipment
Limited UK grey import availability supporting higher replacement values
years.cta: Enquire about Toyota Venza 2021+ hybrid engine availability
years.ticker: Toyota Venza hybrid EGR cooler leak • Toyota Venza 2021 A25A-FXS replacement cost • Toyota Venza hybrid battery cooling fault
enquiries: Toyota Venza hybrid EGR cooler leak
Toyota Venza hybrid battery cooling fault

engineYears.closing: Not sure which year your Toyota Venza was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly - all backed by a minimum 12-month warranty from UK Toyota Venza specialists.

faq.h2: Toyota Venza Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Toyota Venza engine in the UK?
items.answer: Toyota Venza engine replacement costs in the UK typically range from £1,400 for a rebuilt 1AR-FE 2.7L to £5,500 for a rebuilt A25A-FXS hybrid, depending on engine code, condition type and whether supply-only or supply & fit is selected.
keyPoints: Rebuilt 1AR-FE 2.7L petrol: £1,400-£3,200 supply only
Rebuilt 2GR-FE 3.5L V6: £1,800-£4,200 supply only
Rebuilt A25A-FXS hybrid: £2,800-£5,500 supply only
Supply & fit adds £400-£900 depending on location
items.cta: Compare Toyota Venza engine replacement costs now

items.question: Is the Toyota Venza 2GR-FE V6 engine reliable?
items.answer: The 2GR-FE V6 is fundamentally robust but affected by a specific VVT-i actuator issue covered by TSB T-SB-0094-09; replacement with upgraded components resolves the concern long-term.
keyPoints: TSB addresses exhaust VVT-i actuator bolt failure on 2009 models
Ticking noise and DTCs P0014/P0024 are early warning signs
Rebuilt units with revised actuators eliminate recurrence risk
Otherwise excellent longevity when maintained properly
items.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt 2GR-FE Toyota Venza engines

items.question: Why is my Toyota Venza 2.7L burning oil?
items.answer: The 1AR-FE 2.7L can suffer piston ring wear causing oil consumption, particularly on short-trip driven examples; rebuilt engines with revised rings provide a permanent fix.
keyPoints: Consumption typically appears between 70k-120k miles
Blue smoke on acceleration and frequent top-ups are symptoms
PCV valve replacement may help but rarely resolves fully
Rebuilt 1AR-FE with upgraded rings is most reliable solution
items.cta: Find Toyota Venza 1AR-FE engine replacement deals

items.question: What does the P2109 code mean on a Toyota Venza?
items.answer: P2109 indicates throttle actuator control system failure on the 1AR-FE 2.7L, usually requiring throttle body replacement or, on high-mileage examples, consideration of a rebuilt engine.
keyPoints: Code triggers limp mode, poor idle or no-start conditions
Cleaning/reset rarely resolves internal motor failure
Throttle body replacement costs £500-£900 specialist
Rebuilt engine offers better value on higher-mileage Venza
items.cta: Compare Toyota Venza P2109 repair vs replacement options

items.question: Can I replace my Toyota Venza engine with a used unit?
items.answer: Yes, used Toyota Venza engines are available from £700-£3,500 depending on code and mileage, but carry shorter warranties; rebuilt units offer better long-term value for most owners.
keyPoints: Used engines sourced from JDM/USDM donors with mileage verification
Typical warranty 3-6 months vs 12 months on rebuilt units
Compression testing recommended before purchase
Ideal for budget-conscious owners with lower-mileage chassis
items.cta: Check used Toyota Venza engine availability today

items.question: How long does a Toyota Venza engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only engine delivery typically takes 1-5 days depending on condition type; professional fitting adds 1-2 days, with most Venza owners back on the road within a week.
keyPoints: Used engines: 1-3 days dispatch; rebuilt: 3-5 days; hybrid: 7-10 days
Professional fitting requires 6-10 hours labour depending on variant
ECU adaptation and road test included in supply & fit service
Nationwide network ensures minimal downtime for UK owners
items.cta: Find Toyota Venza supply & fit engine deals near you

items.question: Do Toyota Venza hybrid engines come with a warranty?
items.answer: Yes, all hybrid engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty, covering the A25A-FXS powertrain and associated hybrid components.
keyPoints: Warranty covers rebuilt/reconditioned A25A-FXS units
Hybrid system calibration included post-installation
Specialist fitting required to maintain warranty validity
Confirm exact terms with chosen supplier before commitment
items.cta: Enquire about Toyota Venza hybrid engine warranty terms

items.question: What's the difference between reconditioned and rebuilt Toyota Venza engines?
items.answer: Reconditioned Venza engines receive partial refurbishment of wear items; rebuilt units undergo full disassembly with upgraded components addressing known model-specific failure points.
keyPoints: Reconditioned: new gaskets, seals, bearings; suitable for moderate wear
Rebuilt: full machining, upgraded rings/actuators; best for high mileage
Price difference typically £200-£400 favouring rebuilt for long-term value
Both carry 12-month warranty when sourced through EngineMarket
items.cta: Compare reconditioned vs rebuilt Toyota Venza engine prices

items.question: Can a Toyota Venza engine be repaired instead of replaced?
items.answer: Minor issues like VVT-i actuator replacement or throttle body swap can repair specific faults, but rebuilt engines often offer better value when multiple wear items or high mileage are present.
keyPoints: VVT-i actuator repair: £700-£1,300 specialist vs £1,800+ rebuilt engine
Throttle body replacement: £500-£900 vs full rebuild for high-mileage examples
Oil consumption ring job labour-intensive; rebuilt often more economical
Diagnosis-led decision recommended; use our comparison tool for quotes
items.cta: Get tailored Toyota Venza repair vs replacement quotes

items.question: Where can I find a trusted Toyota Venza engine supplier in the UK?
items.answer: EngineMarket connects UK Venza owners with vetted specialists nationwide; every supplier is rated by real customers and offers transparent pricing with warranty protection.
keyPoints: Network includes JDM import specialists familiar with Venza chassis codes
All quotes include engine code verification against your registration
Supply-only and supply & fit options available across the UK
12-month minimum warranty standard on rebuilt/reconditioned units
